info.name: Nova.Shell.Api
info.description:
Reference implementation for Nova platform API services.

Nova.Shell.Api demonstrates all platform conventions: project structure, middleware pipeline, JWT auth, tenant resolution, snake_case JSON, Problem Details error responses, input validation, health checks, Redis caching, distributed locking, service-to-service auth, DbUp migrations, and outbox relay.

This collection is the single source of truth — it serves two purposes:
  1. API reference: run against a locally running Nova.Shell.Api (baseUrl = http://localhost:5100)
  2. Mock server: import into Postman and create a Mock Server

---

Authentication

  Authenticated endpoints (POST /echo, POST /echo/list) need a JWT.
  Use the Nova — Auth (Dev Token Generator) collection to generate access_token first,
  then this collection will automatically attach it as a Bearer token.

  Import: postman/Nova.Auth.postman_collection.json

---

Mock Server Workflow

  1. Import this collection into Postman.
  2. Right-click the collection → Mock Servers → Create Mock Server.
  3. Copy the generated mock URL (e.g. https://abc123.mock.pstmn.io).
  4. Update the baseUrl collection variable to the mock URL.
  5. Send any request — Postman returns the first saved example by default (the 200 case).

  To request a specific response from the mock, add this header:
    x-mock-response-name: <exact name of the saved example>

See src/services/Nova.Shell.Api/docs/shell-api-postman-testing.md for the full testing guide.

exec: // Generates a fresh HS256 token if none exists or the existing one is
// within 60 seconds of expiry. Reads from environment / collection vars.
// Required variables: jwt_secret, tenant_id, company_id, branch_id, user_id
// Skip token generation for anonymous endpoints (hello-world, health/*)
// Reuse the existing token if it is still valid (60 s buffer).
console.log('[Nova.Shell] access_token still valid — skipping refresh.');
} catch (_) { /* malformed token — fall through to regenerate */ }
throw new Error('[Nova.Shell] Missing variable: ' + key +
'. Add it to your active environment or collection variables.');
// Write to collection variables (takes precedence over globals).
// Also write to environment if one is active.

variable.description: Base URL. Local dev: http://localhost:5100. Mock server: replace with Postman Mock URL.
JWT Bearer token. Auto-generated by the collection pre-request script.
Tenant ID to use in POST request bodies. Must match the tenant in the JWT (access_token).

item.name: Diagnostics
item.description: Diagnostic and reference endpoints. No authentication required.

request.description:
Smoke-test endpoint. Returns message, server timestamp (UTC), and correlation_id. No authentication required.

All response fields use snake_case (e.g. correlation_id, not correlationId).

REFERENCE endpoint — demonstrates resilient outbound HttpClient.

Calls GET /health/redis on the configured Services:NovaShell:BaseUrl (default: http://localhost:5100 — itself).
Targets /health/redis (not /health) so the resilience pipeline does not retry on RabbitMQ health failures.

The call goes through the standard resilience pipeline:
  - Retry: up to 3 attempts, exponential back-off with jitter, on 408/429/5xx
  - Circuit breaker: opens after repeated failures; half-open after 30 s
  - Per-attempt timeout: 10 s
  - Total timeout: 30 s

X-Correlation-ID is forwarded from the inbound request to the outbound call.

See HttpPingEndpoint.cs and Nova.Shared.Web/Http/HttpClientExtensions.cs for the implementation.

Executes SELECT code, value, crdate DepDate, [update] UpdatedOn FROM sales97.dbo.pointer against DiagnosticConnections:MsSql.

Returns 503 if DiagnosticConnections:MsSql:Enabled is false (default) or the connection fails.
Set Enabled: true in appsettings.json to activate.

dep_date is a DateOnly ("yyyy-MM-dd") — no time component, no offset.
updated_on is a DateTimeOffset UTC ("yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ss+00:00") — original datetime column treated as UTC.

To get 503 Disabled from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — Disabled
To get 503 Connection failed from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — Connection failed

response.name: 503 Service Unavailable — Connection failed

request.description:
Executes SELECT code, value FROM sales97.pointer against DiagnosticConnections:Postgres.

Returns 503 if DiagnosticConnections:Postgres:Enabled is false (default) or the connection fails.
Set Enabled: true in appsettings.json to activate.

To get 503 Disabled from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — Disabled
To get 503 Connection failed from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — Connection failed

REFERENCE — demonstrates ICacheService.GetOrSetAsync.

First call stores the current UTC timestamp under key nova:test:hello using the ReferenceData profile (Redis, 1 hour TTL).
Subsequent calls return the same cached_at value until the key expires or DELETE /test-cache is called.

If Redis is unavailable, the factory is always invoked (cache miss fallback — app works without cache).

See TestCacheEndpoint.cs for the implementation and usage pattern.

response.name: 200 OK — Cached timestamp (same shape on hit and miss)

request.description:
REFERENCE — demonstrates ICacheService.InvalidateAsync.

Removes the nova:test:hello key from Redis. The next GET /test-cache will run the factory again and store a fresh timestamp.

REFERENCE — demonstrates IDistributedLockService.TryAcquireAsync.

Acquires the lock on nova:test:lock (30 s expiry), runs the critical section (immediate), then releases via Lua script.
Returns 200 if Redis is available and the lock is not held.
Returns 409 Conflict if the lock is already held.

To test contention:
  1. Send GET /test-lock?hold=10 (hangs for 10 s while holding the lock)
  2. Immediately send GET /test-lock from a second tab — returns 409
  3. After 10 s the first call returns 200 and releases the lock

See TestLockEndpoint.cs for the implementation and usage pattern.

response.name: 409 Conflict — Lock already held

item.name: Test Lock — Hold for N seconds (Contention Test)

query.description: Hold the lock for this many seconds (max 30).

request.description:
Acquires the lock and holds it for the specified number of seconds before releasing.
Use to simulate a slow critical section and test contention from a second client.

Send this first, then immediately send GET /test-lock from another Postman tab to see 409 Conflict.

response.name: 200 OK — Lock held for 10 s then released

item.name: Echo (Reference — Validation + Problem Details)
item.description:
POST /echo is the reference endpoint demonstrating all Nova API conventions:
  - RequestContext inheritance (7 standard fields in every POST body)
  - RequestContextValidator.Validate() for standard fields (400)
  - Tenant mismatch check (403)
  - Domain field validation (400)
  - Business rule violation (404 / 422)
  - Unhandled exception → 500 Problem Details (no stack trace)
  - snake_case request binding and response

This folder has a separate request for each scenario so each can be tested independently.
RequireAuthorization() is applied — a valid JWT (access_token) is required for all requests in this folder.

request.description:
All required fields present. Returns 200 with echo, received_at (UTC), correlation_id.

Note: tenant_id in the body must match the tenant in the JWT when RequireAuthorization is added.
Omits all RequestContext fields. RequestContextValidator.Validate() returns errors for tenant_id, company_id, branch_id, user_id. Returns 400 ValidationProblem with per-field errors.

response.name: 400 Validation Failed — Missing context fields

request.description: All context fields present but message is empty string. Returns 400 ValidationProblem with errors.message.
message = 'not-found' triggers the explicit 404 Problem Details return. Demonstrates how to return a not-found response from an endpoint.

response.name: 404 Not Found — Problem Details

request.description: message = 'throw' triggers a deliberate unhandled exception. UseNovaProblemDetails catches it and returns 500 Problem Details. Verify: no stack trace in body, correlation_id and trace_id present.

response.name: 500 Internal Server Error — Unhandled exception

request.description: Hits a route that does not exist. UseNovaProblemDetails (via UseStatusCodePages) formats the 404 as Problem Details. Verify: Content-Type is application/problem+json, correlation_id and trace_id are present.

item.name: Echo List (Reference — Pagination Contract)
item.description:
POST /echo/list demonstrates the Nova pagination contract:
  - Request inherits PagedRequest (7 context fields + page_number + page_size)
  - Validation: RequestContextValidator → TenantMatches → PagedRequestValidator → domain
  - Response: PagedResult<T> with items, total_count, page_number, page_size, total_pages, has_next_page, has_previous_page
  - SQL pattern: dialect.PaginationClause(request.Skip, request.PageSize)

Simulates 47 total items. Adjust page_number and page_size to verify paging behaviour.

request.description: Page 1 of 5. Expect 10 items, total_count=47, total_pages=5, has_next_page=true, has_previous_page=false.

response.name: 200 OK — Page 1 of 5
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    { "id": "item-001", "label": "Echo item 1 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-002", "label": "Echo item 2 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-003", "label": "Echo item 3 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-004", "label": "Echo item 4 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-005", "label": "Echo item 5 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-006", "label": "Echo item 6 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-007", "label": "Echo item 7 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-008", "label": "Echo item 8 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-009", "label": "Echo item 9 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-010", "label": "Echo item 10 (tenant: BLDK)" }
  ],
  "total_count": 47,
  "page_number": 1,
  "page_size": 10,
  "total_pages": 5,
  "has_next_page": true,
  "has_previous_page": false
}

request.description: Page 5 (last page). Items 41–47 = 7 items. has_next_page=false, has_previous_page=true.

response.name: 200 OK — Last page (7 items)
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    { "id": "item-041", "label": "Echo item 41 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-042", "label": "Echo item 42 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-043", "label": "Echo item 43 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-044", "label": "Echo item 44 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-045", "label": "Echo item 45 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-046", "label": "Echo item 46 (tenant: BLDK)" },
    { "id": "item-047", "label": "Echo item 47 (tenant: BLDK)" }
  ],
  "total_count": 47,
  "page_number": 5,
  "page_size": 10,
  "total_pages": 5,
  "has_next_page": false,
  "has_previous_page": true
}

request.description: page_number=0 is invalid (must be 1 or greater). Returns 400 with errors.page_number.
page_size=250 exceeds MaxPageSize=100. Returns 400 with errors.page_size.

item.name: Internal Auth (Reference)
item.description:
Service-to-service JWT authentication. Nova services use a separate InternalJwt scheme (aud=nova-internal) rather than user JWTs (aud=nova-api).

Three endpoints for development and testing:
  GET /test-internal-auth/token     — returns the raw outbound service token for inspection
  GET /test-internal-auth/protected — requires InternalService policy (InternalJwt bearer)
  GET /test-internal-auth/call-self — calls /protected on itself via the internal HTTP client (full round-trip)

Delete these endpoints before cloning for production.
See TestInternalAuthEndpoint.cs and nova-shell-api-guide.md for full documentation.

request.description:
Returns the current outbound service token for inspection. No auth required.

Decode the token at https://jwt.io and verify:
  sub = "nova-shell"  (ServiceName from appsettings.json)
  aud = "nova-internal"
  iss = "https://auth.nova.internal"

Tokens are cached and renewed 30 s before expiry (TokenLifetimeSeconds = 300).

Protected endpoint requiring InternalService policy (InternalJwt scheme).

To call manually:
  1. GET /test-internal-auth/token — copy the token value
  2. Set Authorization: Bearer <token> on this request
  3. Expect 200

Use the {{internal_token}} variable or paste the token directly into the Bearer field.

Calls /test-internal-auth/protected on itself via the nova-shell-internal HTTP client.
Demonstrates the full round-trip: generate token → ServiceTokenHandler attaches it → InternalJwt validates it.

Expect 200 with the same response shape as the protected endpoint.

item.name: Health Checks
item.description:
ASP.NET Core health check endpoints. No authentication required.

  GET /health            — aggregate (Redis + RabbitMQ)
  GET /health/redis      — Redis only (managed by Aspire)
  GET /health/rabbitmq   — RabbitMQ broker connectivity
  GET /health/db/{tenantId} — per-tenant database connectivity (tenant's own connection string)

request.description:
Aggregate health check (Redis + RabbitMQ). 200 if all pass; 503 if any fail.

Note: per-engine DB health is checked per-tenant via GET /health/db/{tenantId}, not here.

To get 503 from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — RabbitMQ unhealthy

response.name: 503 Service Unavailable — RabbitMQ unhealthy

request.description:
Redis-only health check. Managed by Aspire (AddRedisClient). Should always be healthy in dev.

To get 503 from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — Redis down

response.name: 503 Service Unavailable — Redis down

request.description:
RabbitMQ-only health check. Tries to open a connection to the broker configured in appsettings.json → RabbitMq.
Connection timeout: 5 s.

Requires RabbitMQ to be running: docker run -d --name nova-rabbitmq -p 5672:5672 -p 15672:15672 rabbitmq:management

To get 503 from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — RabbitMQ down

response.name: 503 Service Unavailable — RabbitMQ down

request.description:
Per-tenant database connectivity check. Opens a connection using the tenant's own encrypted connection string (not DiagnosticConnections) and executes SELECT 1.

Change the path segment to test a different tenant (e.g. /health/db/client-b).

Returns:
  200 — healthy with latency_ms
  503 — unhealthy with error message
  404 — unknown tenantId

To get 503 from mock: x-mock-response-name: 503 Service Unavailable — DB unreachable
To get 404 from mock: x-mock-response-name: 404 Not Found — Unknown tenant

response.name: 503 Service Unavailable — DB unreachable
response.body:
{
  "tenant_id": "BLDK",
  "db_type": "MsSql",
  "status": "unhealthy",
  "error": "A network-related or instance-specific error occurred while establishing a connection to SQL Server.",
  "latency_ms": 5001
}

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Administrative endpoints. No auth required in dev — add InternalService policy in production.

Migrations are NOT run automatically at startup. Call POST /admin/migrations/run explicitly after each deployment.

request.description:
Runs pending DbUp migrations for ALL tenants in the registry.

Scripts containing commands in migrationpolicy.json apply automatically.
Scripts containing unlisted commands are blocked, logged, and skipped — run manually then journal into SchemaVersions.

Returns a summary per tenant: applied count, blocked count, and details of any blocked scripts.

To get the blocked example from mock: x-mock-response-name: 200 OK — Blocked script

response.name: 200 OK — Blocked script

query.description: Run migrations for this tenant only.

request.description:
Runs pending DbUp migrations for a single tenant only.

Change the tenantId query parameter to target a different tenant.
Returns 404 if the tenantId is not found in the registry.
